metadata.description: Reusable principles for safe initial evaluation and management of orthopaedic trauma.
Structured identification and treatment of immediate life threats before limb-focused care.
Head-to-toe evaluation after immediate life threats have been addressed.
Coordination of fracture care with physiologic stabilization and resuscitation.
Documented motor, sensory, pulse, perfusion, and vascular assessment distal to an injury.
Repeated neurovascular examinations used to detect interval deterioration.
Arterial or venous injury associated with musculoskeletal trauma.

description: Create condition: Traumatic Peripheral Nerve Injury

metadata.description: Peripheral nerve dysfunction caused by trauma, traction, compression, or laceration.
A fracture communicating with the external environment through a traumatic wound.
Classification system for open fractures based on wound, contamination, and soft-tissue injury.

description: Create treatment_principle: Open Fracture Antibiotic Prophylaxis

metadata.description: Prompt antimicrobial prophylaxis selected for the open-fracture injury pattern and contamination context.
Assessment and treatment of tetanus immunization needs after traumatic wounds.

description: Create procedure: Open Fracture Irrigation and Debridement

metadata.description: Operative removal of contamination and devitalized tissue with irrigation of an open fracture wound.
Infection occurring in the setting of a fracture and its treatment.
Pathologic elevation of compartment pressure threatening tissue perfusion and viability.
Serial clinical assessment for pain, passive-stretch pain, swelling, tension, and neurologic change.
Adjunctive invasive pressure measurement when the clinical examination is unreliable or equivocal.
Urgent surgical release of involved osteofascial compartments.
Manipulative restoration of alignment without surgical exposure of the fracture or joint.
Controlled force application used during reduction while protecting soft tissues.
Noncircumferential immobilization that maintains alignment while accommodating swelling.
Repeat examination and imaging after reduction and immobilization.
Imaging used to document alignment after reduction and immobilization.
Skin, subcutaneous tissue, fascia, muscle, and neurovascular structures surrounding an injury.
Protection, reassessment, and staged treatment of injured soft tissues.
Temporary stabilization strategy used when physiology or soft tissues make definitive fixation unsafe.
Percutaneous pin-and-frame stabilization used as a temporary trauma construct.
Definitive fixation strategy selected after adequate physiologic response to resuscitation.
Mechanical environment governing displacement and tissue strain at a fracture site.
Interfragmentary compression strategy minimizing motion at a simple fracture.
Construct strategy permitting controlled micromotion and callus formation.
Relative deformation across a fracture gap under load.
Direct bone healing under conditions of anatomic reduction and high stability.
Indirect healing through callus under relative stability.
Fracture healing that progresses more slowly than expected.
Failure of a fracture to progress to union without further intervention.
Fracture healing in unacceptable alignment.
Interfragmentary compression achieved by screw technique or design.
Internal fixation using a plate-and-screw construct.
Load-sharing internal fixation placed within the medullary canal.

description: Create treatment_principle: Early Postoperative Trauma Care

metadata.description: Immediate postoperative surveillance, mobilization, prophylaxis, and restriction planning after trauma surgery.
Documented limb examination after trauma surgery.
Deep venous thrombosis or pulmonary embolism associated with injury and recovery.
Failure to maintain achieved fracture or joint alignment.
